packets.body:
## Identity

## Edges

- FIGHTS_IN: Battle Beneath the Gods Eye (track_b: Battles)
- DEFEATS: Iron Throne (track_b: Result)
- DEFEATS: Aegon Targaryen (track_b: Result)

## Origins

In [42 AC](wiki:42_AC), Aegon and his sister-wife, Princess [Rhaena](wiki:Rhaena_Targaryen_(daughter_of_Aenys_I)) were in the middle of a [royal progress](wiki:Royal_progress), but found themselves trapped at [Crakehall](wiki:Crakehall) by members of the [Faith of the Seven](wiki:Faith_of_the_Seven), which had [risen in rebellion](wiki:Faith_Militant_uprising) against [House Targaryen](wiki:House_Targaryen). At the same time, Aegon's father, King Aenys, died on [Dragonstone](wiki:Dragonstone). Dowager Queen [Visenya](wiki:Visenya_Targaryen) brought her son and Aenys's half-brother, Prince [Maegor](wiki:Maegor_I_Targaryen), back from his exile in [Essos](wiki:Essos), and he declared himself king ahead of Aegon.(wiki:Aegon_the_Uncrowned's_rebellion.cite_ref-Rtwoiafthe_targaryen_kings:_maegor_i.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_2-0)(wiki:Aegon_the_Uncrowned's_rebellion.cite_ref-Rfabthe_sons_of_the_dragon.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_1-1) When the [Poor Fellows](wiki:Poor_Fellows) who had besieged Crakehall departed to march on [King's Landing](wiki:King's_Landing), Aegon and Rhaena fled to [Casterly Rock](wiki:Casterly_Rock), where Lord [Lyman Lannister](wiki:Lyman_Lannister) protected them by extending [guest right](wiki:Guest_right). There, Aegon's twin daughters [Aerea](wiki:Aerea_Targaryen) and [Rhaella](wiki:Rhaella_Targaryen_(daughter_of_Aegon)) were born the following year.(wiki:Aegon_the_Uncrowned's_rebellion.cite_ref-Rfabthe_sons_of_the_dragon.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_1-2)(wiki:Aegon_the_Uncrowned's_rebellion.cite_ref-Rtwoiafappendix:_targaryen_lineage.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_3-0)(wiki:Aegon_the_Uncrowned's_rebellion.cite_ref-Rtwoiafthe_targaryen_kings:_maegor_i.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_2-1)

After Maegor's coronation, Aegon found himself abandoned by many who he once considered his loyal friends. Throughout the realm, it was whispered that Aegon was weak like his father was, pointing out Aegon had never taken a [dragon](wiki:Dragon) of his own, whereas Maegor had claimed [Balerion the Black Dread](wiki:Balerion). In [43 AC](wiki:43_AC), while Maegor was in [Oldtown](wiki:Oldtown), Rhaena and Aegon took note of his long absence from the capital, and after a long journey managed to successfully infiltrate [King's Landing](wiki:King's_Landing). With several companions aiding them, Aegon and Rhaena entered one of the city gates in a [mule](wiki:Mule) [wagon](wiki:Wagon), concealed in the back under sacks of corn. Once in the city, they were given further aid by several collaborators who had grown weary of Maegor's persecutions. However, Aegon and Rhaena did not seize the [Iron Throne](wiki:Iron_Throne), as they lacked the strength to hold it; they had come for dragons. Rhaena was reunited with her [Dreamfyre](wiki:Dreamfyre), and Aegon finally claimed a dragon, his late father's mount [Quicksilver](wiki:Quicksilver). The pair left the city on dragonback, and returned to the [westerlands](wiki:Westerlands) to raise an army and exact Aegon's claim to the Iron Throne.(wiki:Aegon_the_Uncrowned's_rebellion.cite_ref-Rfabthe_sons_of_the_dragon.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_1-3)

## Aftermath

The battle beneath the Gods Eye in the latter half of [43 AC](wiki:43_AC) was followed by a half a year of trials and executions. Dowager Queen Visenya Targaryen managed to persuade King Maegor to spare some of the rebellious lords. Maegor demanded lands and titles from the rebels, and took [hostages](wiki:Hostage) as well to ensure the loyalty of those he had spared.(wiki:Aegon_the_Uncrowned's_rebellion.cite_ref-Rfabthe_sons_of_the_dragon.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_1-15)

However, Princess Rhaena avoided capture, having remained at Pinkmaiden Castle with her daughters and dragon. Upon hearing of the outcome of the battle, Rhaena gathered her daughters and fled on dragonback from the riverlands, fearing her uncle's wroth. While first arriving at Lannisport, she would later flee to [Faircastle](wiki:Faircastle) on [Fair Isle](wiki:Fair_Isle), the seat of Lord [Marq Farman](wiki:Marq_Farman). His father and brother had perished in the battle, but Farman offered Rhaena sanctuary beneath his roof.(wiki:Aegon_the_Uncrowned's_rebellion.cite_ref-Rfabthe_sons_of_the_dragon.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_1-16)

Later, Maegor married Lord Alan Tarbeck's widow, Lady [Jeyne Westerling](wiki:Jeyne_Westerling_(wife_of_Maegor_I)), along with Princess Rhaena, in a ceremony where the two women and [another widow](wiki:Elinor_Costayne) were called the "[Black Brides](wiki:Black_Brides)".(wiki:Aegon_the_Uncrowned's_rebellion.cite_ref-Rfabthe_sons_of_the_dragon.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_1-17)

During the [Faith Militant uprising](wiki:Faith_Militant_uprising), [Poor Fellows](wiki:Poor_Fellows) led by Ser [Joffrey Doggett](wiki:Joffrey_Doggett) targeted and [assassinated](wiki:Assassin) some of Maegor's loyalists that had participated in the battle, including Old Lord Merryweather, the heir of Lord Peake, and Ser Davos Darklyn's aged father.(wiki:Aegon_the_Uncrowned's_rebellion.cite_ref-Rfabthe_sons_of_the_dragon.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_1-18)

## Identity
The kingsroad incident on the Trident's south bank in which Arya Stark, practicing swords with the butcher's boy Mycah, was confronted by Prince Joffrey. Joffrey cut Mycah with Lion's Tooth; Arya struck Joffrey with a stick; Nymeria bit Joffrey's arm; Arya threw Joffrey's sword into the river. Royal justice followed at Castle Darry: Cersei demanded a direwolf's life, Ned executed Lady in Nymeria's stead, and Sandor Clegane returned with Mycah's body. Cause of the Lady-direwolf rift and the first major political confrontation between House Stark and House Lannister on the way south.

## Identity

## Edges


## Origins

### Prelude[ ]

Lord [Tywin Lannister](wiki:Tywin_Lannister), [Hand of the King](wiki:Hand_of_the_King) to [Aerys II Targaryen](wiki:Aerys_II_Targaryen), tried to betroth his daughter [Cersei](wiki:Cersei_Lannister) to [Rhaegar Targaryen](wiki:Rhaegar_Targaryen), [Prince of Dragonstone](wiki:Prince_of_Dragonstone), at the [tourney in Lannisport](wiki:Tournament_in_honor_of_Viserys's_birth) in honor of [Viserys Targaryen](wiki:Viserys_Targaryen)'s birth in [276 AC](wiki:Years_after_Aegon's_Conquest), but King Aerys refused to marry "his heir to his servant's daughter".(wiki:Wedding_of_Robert_I_Baratheon_and_Cersei_Lannister.cite_ref-Raffc24.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_3-0) Rhaegar instead married Princess [Elia Martell](wiki:Elia_Martell) of [Dorne](wiki:Dorne) in [279 AC](wiki:Years_after_Aegon's_Conquest).(wiki:Wedding_of_Robert_I_Baratheon_and_Cersei_Lannister.cite_ref-Rtwoiafthe_targaryen_kings:_aerys_ii.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_4-0)
In [282 AC](wiki:282_AC), Rhaegar abducted [Lyanna Stark](wiki:Lyanna_Stark), who was betrothed to [Robert Baratheon](wiki:Robert_I_Baratheon), [Lord of Storm's End](wiki:Lord_of_Storm's_End). During [Robert's Rebellion](wiki:Robert's_Rebellion) against [House Targaryen](wiki:House_Targaryen), Rhaegar was slain by Robert in the [Battle of the Trident](wiki:Battle_of_the_Trident).(wiki:Wedding_of_Robert_I_Baratheon_and_Cersei_Lannister.cite_ref-Ragot4.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_5-0) In order to prove his loyalty to Robert,(wiki:Wedding_of_Robert_I_Baratheon_and_Cersei_Lannister.cite_ref-Rasos53.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_6-0) Tywin led the [Sack of King's Landing](wiki:Sack_of_King's_Landing), during which Ser [Jaime Lannister](wiki:Jaime_Lannister) of the [Kingsguard](wiki:Kingsguard), his son and Cersei's twin, slew Aerys in the [throne room](wiki:Throne_room).(wiki:Wedding_of_Robert_I_Baratheon_and_Cersei_Lannister.cite_ref-Ragot12.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_7-0) According to a semi-canon source, Lyanna died at the [tower of joy](wiki:Tower_of_joy) in [Dorne](wiki:Dorne).(wiki:Wedding_of_Robert_I_Baratheon_and_Cersei_Lannister.cite_ref-Rawoiaflyanna_stark.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_8-0)
Robert claimed the [Iron Throne](wiki:Iron_Throne) and [succeeded Aerys as king](wiki:Coronation_of_Robert_I_Baratheon) in [283 AC](wiki:283_AC).(wiki:Wedding_of_Robert_I_Baratheon_and_Cersei_Lannister.cite_ref-Rtwoiafappendix:_reign_of_the_kings.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_9-0) [Jon Arryn](wiki:Jon_Arryn), the new Hand, advised Robert to marry Cersei to bind [House Baratheon of King's Landing](wiki:House_Baratheon_of_King's_Landing) with powerful [House Lannister](wiki:House_Lannister).(wiki:Wedding_of_Robert_I_Baratheon_and_Cersei_Lannister.cite_ref-Ragot30.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_10-0)

### Wedding[ ]

According to Maester [Yandel](wiki:Yandel), [Robert I Baratheon](wiki:Robert_I_Baratheon)'s first act as king was to marry [Cersei Lannister](wiki:Cersei_Lannister).(wiki:Wedding_of_Robert_I_Baratheon_and_Cersei_Lannister.cite_ref-Rtwoiafthe_glorious_reign.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_11-0) Ser [Jaime Lannister](wiki:Jaime_Lannister), having been pardoned by Robert and allowed to remain in the [Kingsguard](wiki:Kingsguard), served in the honor guard which brought his sister to [King's Landing](wiki:King's_Landing);(wiki:Wedding_of_Robert_I_Baratheon_and_Cersei_Lannister.cite_ref-Rasos67.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_12-0) Robert was unaware the twins had an [incestuous](wiki:Incest) relationship.(wiki:Wedding_of_Robert_I_Baratheon_and_Cersei_Lannister.cite_ref-Racok31.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_13-0)(wiki:Wedding_of_Robert_I_Baratheon_and_Cersei_Lannister.cite_ref-Rasos11.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_14-0) Robert replaced the [skulls of the Targaryen dragons](wiki:Skulls_of_the_Targaryen_dragons) in the [throne room](wiki:Great_Hall_(Red_Keep)) of the [Red Keep](wiki:Red_Keep) with banners and tapestries. Hoping to see the skulls when he arrived for the wedding, [Tyrion Lannister](wiki:Tyrion_Lannister), Cersei's youngest brother, eventually found them in a cellar beneath the castle.(wiki:Wedding_of_Robert_I_Baratheon_and_Cersei_Lannister.cite_ref-Ragot13.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_15-0)
Cersei and Jaime had sex on the morning of the wedding.(wiki:Wedding_of_Robert_I_Baratheon_and_Cersei_Lannister.cite_ref-Radwd54.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_16-0) Robert and Cersei were wed later in the day in the [Great Sept of Baelor](wiki:Great_Sept_of_Baelor).(wiki:Wedding_of_Robert_I_Baratheon_and_Cersei_Lannister.cite_ref-Raffc28.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_2-1) During the changing of the cloaks, Robert draped Cersei with a heavy [cloth-of-gold](wiki:Cloth-of-gold) cloak with the [stag](wiki:Stag) of [House Baratheon](wiki:House_Baratheon) depicted with beads of [onyx](wiki:Onyx).(wiki:Wedding_of_Robert_I_Baratheon_and_Cersei_Lannister.cite_ref-Raffc12.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_17-0) Thousands cheered at the plaza outside when the couple left the sept, though Cersei noticed that her brother Jaime was unhappy.(wiki:Wedding_of_Robert_I_Baratheon_and_Cersei_Lannister.cite_ref-Raffc28.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_2-2) When Robert and Cersei slept together for the first time the night of the wedding feast, the drunken king whispered [Lyanna Stark](wiki:Lyanna_Stark)'s name instead of his bride's.(wiki:Wedding_of_Robert_I_Baratheon_and_Cersei_Lannister.cite_ref-Ragot45.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_18-0)
Jaime was champion in the tourney held at the capital for the wedding.(wiki:Wedding_of_Robert_I_Baratheon_and_Cersei_Lannister.cite_ref-Rasos67.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_12-1) Cersei's uncle, [Gerion Lannister](wiki:Gerion_Lannister), gave King Robert a gilded dagger with an [ivory](wiki:Ivory) grip and a [sapphire](wiki:Sapphire) pommel as a wedding gift.(wiki:Wedding_of_Robert_I_Baratheon_and_Cersei_Lannister.cite_ref-Rasos32.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_19-0) Robert appointed Ser [Ilyn Payne](wiki:Ilyn_Payne), whose tongue had been removed by the late King [Aerys II Targaryen](wiki:Aerys_II_Targaryen), as the [King's Justice](wiki:King's_Justice) as his own gift for Tywin.(wiki:Wedding_of_Robert_I_Baratheon_and_Cersei_Lannister.cite_ref-Raffc27.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_20-0)

### Aftermath[ ]

Two of Robert's [Estermont](wiki:House_Estermont) uncles remained at court in King's Landing for half a year following the wedding. Robert insisted on repaying the visit, with Queen Cersei and members of his court accompanying him for a two-week stay at [Greenstone](wiki:Greenstone). While there, Cersei suspected that Robert was bedding a [widowed cousin](wiki:Estermont_(cousin_of_Robert)) he used to play with as a child, and the queen had Jaime follow Robert to confirm her suspicions. Cersei and Jaime slept together at Greenstone, and Cersei likes to believe that was the night their eldest child, Prince [Joffrey Baratheon](wiki:Joffrey_Baratheon), was conceived.(wiki:Wedding_of_Robert_I_Baratheon_and_Cersei_Lannister.cite_ref-Raffc24.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_3-1)
The marriage between Robert and Cersei continued to deteriorate over the years,(wiki:Wedding_of_Robert_I_Baratheon_and_Cersei_Lannister.cite_ref-Raffc32.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_21-0) and Cersei and Jaime continued their clandestine incest.(wiki:Wedding_of_Robert_I_Baratheon_and_Cersei_Lannister.cite_ref-Raffc28.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_2-3) Robert believes himself to be the father of Joffrey and the prince's siblings, Princess [Myrcella](wiki:Myrcella_Baratheon) and Prince [Tommen Baratheon](wiki:Tommen_Baratheon).(wiki:Wedding_of_Robert_I_Baratheon_and_Cersei_Lannister.cite_ref-Ragot47.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_22-0)

## Narrative Arc

### A Game of Thrones[ ]

King [Robert I Baratheon](wik

...[TRUNCATED — body exceeds 8000 char cap]...

## Identity

## Edges

- FIGHTS_IN: Robert's Rebellion (track_b: Conflict)
- DEFEATS: Rebel victory - Robert wins three battles in a single day. (track_b: Result)

## Origins

After the [taking of Gulltown](wiki:Taking_of_Gulltown), Lord [Robert Baratheon](wiki:Robert_I_Baratheon) returned to [Storm's End](wiki:Storm's_End) and called his bannermen in rebellion against King [Aerys II Targaryen](wiki:Aerys_II_Targaryen). However, some of the storm lords remained loyal to Aerys and, encouraged by Lord [Owen Merryweather](wiki:Owen_Merryweather),(wiki:Battles_at_Summerhall.cite_ref-Rtwoiaf_the_fall_of_the_dragons:_robert.27s_rebellion.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_1-0) the [Hand of the King](wiki:Hand_of_the_King), they rejected Robert's commands. Lords [Cafferen](wiki:Lord_Cafferen), [Fell](wiki:Lord_Fell), and [Grandison](wiki:Lord_Grandison) were going to join forces at [Summerhall](wiki:Summerhall), a ruined castle of [House Targaryen](wiki:House_Targaryen) in the [Dornish Marches](wiki:Dornish_Marches), and then march on Storm's End to confront their liege lord. Robert, catching wind of it, raced with his forces to meet them.

## Narrative Arc

Not much is known about the battle, only that Robert managed to arrive first and won three battles in a single day, defeating the forces of the three loyalist houses in turn as they arrived. Additionally, Robert killed [Lord Fell](wiki:Lord_Fell) in single combat.(wiki:Battles_at_Summerhall.cite_ref-Rasos36.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_2-0)

## Aftermath

Robert consolidated his power in the [stormlands](wiki:Stormlands), winning the support of the rest of the storm lords, which allowed him to march towards the [Reach](wiki:Reach) with no enemy forces loose in his rear.

The late Lord Fell's son, [Silveraxe](wiki:Silveraxe), was captured, as were [Lord Grandison](wiki:Lord_Grandison) and [Lord Cafferen](wiki:Lord_Cafferen) were captured. Taken to Storm's End as prisoners, the three joined the rebel cause after some time spent in captivity. Lord [Randyll Tarly](wiki:Randyll_Tarly) killed Cafferen in the [battle of Ashford](wiki:Battle_of_Ashford), while Grandison died after the war from wounds taken in the [Battle of the Trident](wiki:Battle_of_the_Trident).(wiki:Battles_at_Summerhall.cite_ref-Rasos36.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_2-1)(wiki:Battles_at_Summerhall.cite_ref-Rasos54.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_3-0)

## Identity

## Edges


## Origins

### Robert's Rebellion[ ]

In [282 AC](wiki:282_AC), [Rhaegar Targaryen](wiki:Rhaegar_Targaryen), [Prince of Dragonstone](wiki:Prince_of_Dragonstone), abducted [Lyanna Stark](wiki:Lyanna_Stark), who was betrothed to [Robert Baratheon](wiki:Robert_I_Baratheon), [Lord of Storm's End](wiki:Lord_of_Storm's_End).(wiki:Coronation_of_Robert_I_Baratheon.cite_ref-Ragot66.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_2-0) Lyanna's brother, [Brandon Stark](wiki:Brandon_Stark), was imprisoned by King [Aerys II Targaryen](wiki:Aerys_II_Targaryen) when he rode to the [Red Keep](wiki:Red_Keep) to confront Rhaegar, and the Mad King then executed Brandon and his father, Lord [Rickard Stark](wiki:Rickard_Stark).(wiki:Coronation_of_Robert_I_Baratheon.cite_ref-Racok55.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_3-0) Aerys next sent an order to [Jon Arryn](wiki:Jon_Arryn), [Lord of the Eyrie](wiki:Lord_of_the_Eyrie), to behead Robert and [Eddard Stark](wiki:Eddard_Stark), now [Lord of Winterfell](wiki:Lord_of_Winterfell) after the death of his father Rickard. Jon refused to kill his former wards, however, and the [Seven Kingdoms](wiki:Seven_Kingdoms) plunged into civil war with [Robert's Rebellion](wiki:Robert's_Rebellion).(wiki:Coronation_of_Robert_I_Baratheon.cite_ref-Radwd9.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_4-0)
Robert announced his intention to claim the [Iron Throne](wiki:Iron_Throne),(wiki:Coronation_of_Robert_I_Baratheon.cite_ref-Rawoiafrobert_baratheon.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_5-0)(wiki:Coronation_of_Robert_I_Baratheon.cite_ref-6) and he slew Rhaegar in the [Battle of the Trident](wiki:Battle_of_the_Trident).(wiki:Coronation_of_Robert_I_Baratheon.cite_ref-Ragot4.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_7-0) Lord [Tywin Lannister](wiki:Tywin_Lannister) led a host to [King's Landing](wiki:King's_Landing) to ostensibly aid King Aerys, but he had his men attack the Targaryen loyalists after being admitted to the capital. Tywin's eldest son, Ser [Jaime Lannister](wiki:Jaime_Lannister) of the [Kingsguard](wiki:Kingsguard), killed Aerys, the king he was sworn to protect, during the [Sack of King's Landing](wiki:Sack_of_King's_Landing)(wiki:Coronation_of_Robert_I_Baratheon.cite_ref-Ragot12.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_8-0) in [283 AC](wiki:283_AC).(wiki:Coronation_of_Robert_I_Baratheon.cite_ref-Radwdappendix.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_9-0)
Tywin presented the bodies of Rhaegar's wife, [Elia Martell](wiki:Elia_Martell), and children, Princess [Rhaenys](wiki:Rhaenys_Targaryen_(daughter_of_Rhaegar)) and Prince [Aegon Targaryen](wiki:Aegon_Targaryen_(son_of_Rhaegar)), to Robert as tokens of fealty by [House Lannister](wiki:House_Lannister). While Robert accepted their deaths as consequences of war, the murders outraged Lord Stark,(wiki:Coronation_of_Robert_I_Baratheon.cite_ref-Ragot12.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_8-1) and Eddard departed King's Landing to lift the [siege of Storm's End](wiki:Siege_of_Storm's_End) to the south.(wiki:Coronation_of_Robert_I_Baratheon.cite_ref-Racokprologue.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_10-0) Their shared grief at [Lyanna's passing](wiki:Tower_of_joy) later reconciled Robert and Eddard.(wiki:Coronation_of_Robert_I_Baratheon.cite_ref-Ragot12.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_8-2)

### Coronation[ ]

Ser [Jaime Lannister](wiki:Jaime_Lannister), Lord [Varys](wiki:Varys), and Grand Maester [Pycelle](wiki:Pycelle) knelt at King [Robert I Baratheon](wiki:Robert_I_Baratheon)'s feet during his coronation so they could be pardoned for having served the Mad King [Aerys II Targaryen](wiki:Aerys_II_Targaryen) and return to royal service.(wiki:Coronation_of_Robert_I_Baratheon.cite_ref-Racok55.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_3-1)
During his coronation feast, Robert spoke to Jaime about his being known as "Kingslayer" for having killed Aerys, joking it should not become a habit.(wiki:Coronation_of_Robert_I_Baratheon.cite_ref-Rasos37.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_11-0)
Robert's first act as king was to take as his queen [Cersei Lannister](wiki:Cersei_Lannister), Jaime's twin and the daughter of Lord [Tywin Lannister](wiki:Tywin_Lannister),(wiki:Coronation_of_Robert_I_Baratheon.cite_ref-Rtwoiafthe_glorious_reign.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_12-0) and he had the [skulls of the Targaryen dragons](wiki:Skulls_of_the_Targaryen_dragons) removed from the [throne room](wiki:Great_Hall_(Red_Keep)) before the wedding.(wiki:Coronation_of_Robert_I_Baratheon.cite_ref-Ragot13.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_13-0)(wiki:Coronation_of_Robert_I_Baratheon.cite_ref-Rasos54.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_14-0)

## Quotes

Robert: Damn you, Ned Stark. You and [Jon Arryn](wiki:Jon_Arryn), I loved you both. What have you done to me? You were the one should have been king, you or Jon. Eddard: You had the better claim, Your Grace.(wiki:Coronation_of_Robert_I_Baratheon.cite_ref-Ragot30.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_15-0)
I remember [Robert](wiki:Robert_I_Baratheon) as he was the day he took [the throne](wiki:Iron_Throne), every inch a king.(wiki:Coronation_of_Robert_I_Baratheon.cite_ref-Ragot45.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_16-0)
I tell you this—[Robert](wiki:Robert_I_Baratheon) was never the same after he put on that crown. Some men are like swords, made for fighting. Hang them up and they go to rust.(wiki:Coronation_of_Robert_I_Baratheon.cite_ref-Racok6.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_17-0)
At [Robert's](wiki:Robert_I_Baratheon) coronation, I was made to kneel at the royal feet beside Grand Maester [Pycelle](wiki:Pycelle) and [Varys](wiki:Varys) the eunuch, so that he might forgive us our crimes before he took us into his service.(wiki:Coronation_of_Robert_I_Baratheon.cite_ref-Racok55.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_3-2)

## Origins

Previously a free people ruled by their own [kings](wiki:King_of_the_Three_Sisters), the islands of the Three Sisters were conquered by [northmen](wiki:North) who had tired of their [pirating](wiki:Pirate) and pillaging. According to chronicles written by Sistermen and men of the [Vale of Arryn](wiki:Vale_of_Arryn), the northmen committed numerous atrocities. Supposedly, they killed children and cooked them in pots, disemboweled men and wound their entrails around spits, executed three thousand warriors in a single day at the [Headman's Mount](wiki:Headman's_Mount), and [Belthasar Bolton](wiki:Belthasar_Bolton) made a "[Pink Pavilion](wiki:Pink_Pavilion)" out of the [flayed](wiki:Flaying) skins of a hundred Sistermen. Northern accounts of the war do not mention these actions.(wiki:Rape_of_the_Three_Sisters.cite_ref-Rtwoiaf_the_vale.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_2-1)

King [Theon Stark](wiki:Theon_Stark) once conquered the islands,(wiki:Rape_of_the_Three_Sisters.cite_ref-Rtwoiaf_the_north:_the_kings_of_winter.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_3-0) although it is unknown if this was part of the Rape of the Three Sisters or during a different time.

## Aftermath

In response to the invasion, the Sistermen turned to [Mathos II Arryn](wiki:Mathos_II_Arryn), [King of Mountain and Vale](wiki:King_of_Mountain_and_Vale), for assistance in ousting the northmen. In return, the previously independent Sistermen bent the knee to the [Eyrie](wiki:Eyrie). The Rape of the Three Sisters became the first act in a thousand years of conflict between the [Starks](wiki:House_Stark) and [Arryns](wiki:House_Arryn) over the islands, known as the [War Across the Water](wiki:War_Across_the_Water), or the Worthless War.(wiki:Rape_of_the_Three_Sisters.cite_ref-Radwd9.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_1-1)
